rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL D. BRIARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/9/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of the amendment has been filed. As pointed out in Examiner's Notes emailed to the attorney on 10/2/25, the ONLY PROPER FORM OF NOTICE is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (4) The address for Lori Barrows Hoag shown in paragraph 2 of the amendment is not the address to which notice was mailed to her on 8/14/25. (5) Is the administrator alleging that she has verified the heirship information? Doulas Bohne's declaration is not hers (the fiduciary). (6) The family chart and supporting documents attached to the amendment do not appear to be complete. Sharon Briard Lindkvist's birth certificate and marriage certificate do not identify her parents. Robert Lee Brewer's obituary shows that he was married to Bertha Edwards Brewer, not Grace Elizabeth Lechner Barrows, and Kenneth L. Brewer's birth certificate does not identify his parents. Attachment 18 (ostensibly Lori A. Barrows Hoag's birth certificate) is partially illegible. Why does Kathryn Philpott take, not her mother Laura Lechner Tryon? Floyd Wesley Briard's obituary shows his date of death as 12/13/61, not 12/12/61. (7) Please file proof of the sale of the firearms by the dealers (receipt of the sale proceeds by the estate). (8) The proposed distribution is the same as in the petition - not the one reflecting the corrected statutory compensation. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/23/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Modification Of Irrevocable Trust (Prbate Code 15403, 15408,15409, And 17200
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] The trust at paragraph 6.5 provides for more remote issue to take if the children do not survive the surviving settlor. Do the children have issue? If so, a guardian ad litem should be appointed to represent their interests or the interests of any unborn children/issue. The trust at paragraph 6.3 provides for a QDOT trust, but the petition does not address the surviving settlor's citizenship status. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/24/2025 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 10/24/2025).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLES R. KRAMLICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit will. No appearance required. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 06/07/2027. MICHAEL KASOLAS is appointed probate referee. DISCLOSURE: The assigned probate referee is married to a legal advisory staff member of this court.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSE RICO DELGADO JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/9/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An AMENDED petition must be filed to correct the date of death. (2) Item 3g(2) of the petition should have been completed, as the petitioner is related to the decedent. (3) Why is item 3g(4) checked? Who was the former personal representative? Please provide courtesy copies of the amended petition and proof of publication of notice of the amended petition to the assigned examiner by 2/23/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The proposed fiduciary or a Court-appointed fiduciary must make her own declarations or allegations, not just verify her attorney's.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS JAMES HART
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/17/25. Issues are as follows: 1. A copy of the lost will must be filed in the same way as an original will is filed. 2. Attachment 3f(3) was not attached to the petition, which must discuss the presumption under Probate Code 6124. ** Please address the issues above completely and comprehensibly. Please DO NOT reference these notes, as they are not part of the record. Please do not file a fully amended petition unless necessary or requested. ** All supplemental declarations and amendments must be verified by the petitioner, unless indicated otherwise. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends (including publication). ** All documents filed in response to these notes should be filed and viewable on the register of actions by 12/01/25. Examiner's Notes will be emailed to the attorney upon availability, provided an email address is included in a subsequently filed pleading.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HERMILO ELEPANO RODIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance by counsel required. Proposed Order (VS-109, Page 1 only) must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
